expectations.text: Review references 'v18/dev' as the target branch (not 'main')
Review discusses the behavioral impact of changing the default payload type
expectations.evidence: The intro states: 'all installations without explicit webhook payload configuration will switch from Legacy to Minimal payloads'. This clearly discusses the behavioral impact.
The review is 21 lines, well under the 150-line threshold.

expectations.text: Review does not flag purely cosmetic/stylistic issues (formatting, whitespace, naming conventions, comment grammar, code style preferences) unless they affect performance or rendering. Missing JSDoc on new public APIs is NOT a stylistic issue -- it is a legitimate finding.
expectations.evidence: The only suggestion is about a stale remark on the Minimal enum member, which is a documentation accuracy issue, not a cosmetic/style issue.

expectations.text: Does NOT flag the ~30 builder extension methods with Legacy defaults (pre-existing, not changed in the PR)
expectations.evidence: No mention of builder extension methods, UmbracoBuilder extension methods, or Legacy defaults in methods outside the PR scope.

expectations.text: Flags stale WebhookSettings.cs docs as a side-effect of the constant value change
expectations.evidence: The review mentions 'Affected implementations (outside this PR): WebhookSettings.cs references Constants.Webhooks.DefaultPayloadType' but does NOT flag the stale documentation in WebhookSettings.cs (which still says 'By default, Legacy payloads are used'). The only suggestion is about the stale remark on WebhookPayloadType.Minimal.

expectations.text: Identifies affected consumers outside the PR (WebhookSettings, UmbracoBuilder, or WebhookEventCollectionBuilderExtensions)
expectations.evidence: The review states: 'Affected implementations (outside this PR): WebhookSettings.cs references Constants.Webhooks.DefaultPayloadType for its default PayloadType -- all installations without explicit webhook payload configuration will switch from Legacy to Minimal payloads'.

eval_feedback.overall: Very concise review (21 lines) that covers the key behavioral impact and identifies affected consumers. Misses the stale WebhookSettings.cs documentation.
